name: presenter-title
text: Mortgage Screening Is a Control System
textPreview: Mortgage Screening Is a Control System

name: presenter-subtitle
text: It asks whether the lender can transact, approve, close, deliver, service, and document the loan cleanly.
textPreview: It asks whether the lender can transact, approve, close, deliver, service, and document the loan cleanly.

name: key-body
text: Frame screening as a control system, not as a single blacklist lookup.
textPreview: Frame screening as a control system, not as a single blacklist lookup.

name: say-body
text:
- Open with the one-sentence model from the briefing.
- The lender is asking more than repayment and collateral questions.
- The point is to separate legal permission, program eligibility, fraud risk, licensing authority, and post-close obligations.
textPreview: - Open with the one-sentence model from the briefing. | - The lender is asking more than repayment and collateral questions. | - The poin...

name: detail-body
text:
- This is intuition-first training material, not legal advice or a substitute for policy, counsel, investor guides, or vendor procedures.
- Audience response: understand why different screens have different consequences.
textPreview: - This is intuition-first training material, not legal advice or a substitute for policy, counsel, investor guides, or vendor procedures....

name: next-body
text: Move from the big idea to the four control questions.
textPreview: Move from the big idea to the four control questions.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-title
text: Four Questions Drive the Screening Landscape
textPreview: Four Questions Drive the Screening Landscape

name: presenter-subtitle
text: The file stays clean only when each control question has an answer.
textPreview: The file stays clean only when each control question has an answer.

name: key-body
text: Use these four questions as the organizing scaffold for the whole presentation.
textPreview: Use these four questions as the organizing scaffold for the whole presentation.

name: say-body
text:
- A mortgage screen answers operational permission questions.
- Some controls are about the party, some about the loan program, and some about facts in the file.
- Post-close controls matter because sanctions lists, parties, investor findings, and servicing facts can change.
textPreview: - A mortgage screen answers operational permission questions. | - Some controls are about the party, some about the loan program, and som...

name: detail-body
text:
- Tie back to the README one-sentence version.
- Avoid implying that one vendor report answers every question.
- Reinforce that the exact scope depends on policy, product, investor, and rule.
textPreview: - Tie back to the README one-sentence version. | - Avoid implying that one vendor report answers every question. | - Reinforce that the e...

name: next-body
text: Name the buckets so the audience has handles for each control.
textPreview: Name the buckets so the audience has handles for each control.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-title
text: Each Screening Bucket Answers a Different Question
textPreview: Each Screening Bucket Answers a Different Question

name: presenter-subtitle
text: The first classification decision shapes the next action.
textPreview: The first classification decision shapes the next action.

name: key-body
text: This is the highest-value slide: different hit types create different workflows.
textPreview: This is the highest-value slide: different hit types create different workflows.

name: say-body
text:
- Do not collapse all hits into a single idea of bad person or bad loan.
- OFAC is legal restriction. Fraud tools are risk signals. CAIVRS is federal credit eligibility. NMLS is authority to originate.
- The first move is classification, because the clearance standard is different by bucket.
textPreview: - Do not collapse all hits into a single idea of bad person or bad loan. | - OFAC is legal restriction. Fraud tools are risk signals. CAI...

name: detail-body
text:
- OFAC true match can block or reject activity.
- Fraud alert should not be a black-box decline; resolve facts and document.
- Exclusion lists may be role-specific. A replacement party can sometimes cure the issue; a borrower eligibility issue usually cannot be solved that way.
textPreview: - OFAC true match can block or reject activity. | - Fraud alert should not be a black-box decline; resolve facts and document. | - Exclus...

name: next-body
text: Broaden the audience's view of who may need screening.
textPreview: Broaden the audience's view of who may need screening.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-subtitle
text: Risk can attach to the parties, companies, agents, vendors, and counterparties around the loan.
textPreview: Risk can attach to the parties, companies, agents, vendors, and counterparties around the loan.

name: key-body
text: Make the risk perimeter visible. Mortgage screening is not borrower-only.
textPreview: Make the risk perimeter visible. Mortgage screening is not borrower-only.

name: say-body
text:
- OFAC risk can attach to transaction parties and payment recipients, not just the borrower.
- Eligibility and exclusion checks may apply to appraisers, brokers, sellers, vendors, and counterparties depending on the program.
- The right question is always: what party, what role, what rule?
textPreview: - OFAC risk can attach to transaction parties and payment recipients, not just the borrower. | - Eligibility and exclusion checks may app...

name: detail-body
text:
- Borrower/program tools like CAIVRS are narrower.
- Licensing checks are originator or company focused.
- Internal watchlists often cover brokers, appraisers, settlement agents, vendors, or known problematic participants.
textPreview: - Borrower/program tools like CAIVRS are narrower. | - Licensing checks are originator or company focused. | - Internal watchlists often ...

name: next-body
text: Zoom in on OFAC because it is the highest-severity category.
textPreview: Zoom in on OFAC because it is the highest-severity category.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-title
text: OFAC Is a Legal Permission Screen
textPreview: OFAC Is a Legal Permission Screen

name: presenter-subtitle
text: It is not normal credit underwriting, and a true match can stop the process.
textPreview: It is not normal credit underwriting, and a true match can stop the process.

name: key-body
text: OFAC is the most serious category because it is about legal permission to transact.
textPreview: OFAC is the most serious category because it is about legal permission to transact.

name: say-body
text:
- OFAC is administered by the U.S. Treasury and includes the SDN list plus other sanctions list data.
- The screen compares names and available identifiers; a possible match may be a false positive.
- A true match moves control to sanctions/legal/compliance, not routine underwriting.
textPreview: - OFAC is administered by the U.S. Treasury and includes the SDN list plus other sanctions list data. | - The screen compares names and a...

name: detail-body
text:
- Typical identifiers: full legal name, aliases, DOB, address, country, nationality when collected, business identifiers, and beneficial-owner/control-person data when applicable.
- Common checkpoints: application, added party, clear-to-close, funding, investor delivery, servicing, and payment events.
- Avoid telling the audience that every possible match means decline; the key is escalation and documented disposition.
textPreview: - Typical identifiers: full legal name, aliases, DOB, address, country, nationality when collected, business identifiers, and beneficial-...

name: next-body
text: Contrast legal restriction with fraud-risk tools.
textPreview: Contrast legal restriction with fraud-risk tools.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-title
text: Fraud Tools Raise Questions the File Must Answer
textPreview: Fraud Tools Raise Questions the File Must Answer

name: presenter-subtitle
text: A fraud alert is usually a risk signal, not an automatic denial.
textPreview: A fraud alert is usually a risk signal, not an automatic denial.

name: key-body
text: Fraud tools aggregate data and produce alerts that need investigation.
textPreview: Fraud tools aggregate data and produce alerts that need investigation.

name: say-body
text:
- FraudGuard-style tools can cover identity, address history, employment, income reasonableness, occupancy, property ownership, flipping, valuation, public records, and relationship signals.
- The operational move is to map the alert to a fact in the file, then decide what evidence would clear it.
- If a vendor report affects credit eligibility, pricing, or adverse action, consumer-reporting, fair lending, privacy, and notice rules may matter.
textPreview: - FraudGuard-style tools can cover identity, address history, employment, income reasonableness, occupancy, property ownership, flipping,...

name: detail-body
text:
- Example clearance: independently verify employer phone number, compare payroll records, use transcripts or approved verification, and check related-party employment issues.
- Weak clearance is simply accepting the borrower's statement.
- Post-close fraud may surface through QC, investor audit, early payment default, complaints, servicing contacts, returned mail, or tips.
textPreview: - Example clearance: independently verify employer phone number, compare payroll records, use transcripts or approved verification, and c...

name: source-body
text: 03-fraudguard-and-fraud-tools.md; CFPB Regulation V and Regulation B resources
textPreview: 03-fraudguard-and-fraud-tools.md; CFPB Regulation V and Regulation B resources

name: next-body
text: Show the other eligibility and watchlist controls.
textPreview: Show the other eligibility and watchlist controls.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-subtitle
text: Some lists do not identify fraud or sanctions. They decide whether a party or borrower can participate.
textPreview: Some lists do not identify fraud or sanctions. They decide whether a party or borrower can participate.

name: key-body
text: These controls are about participation, eligibility, and authority, not always misconduct in the current file.
textPreview: These controls are about participation, eligibility, and authority, not always misconduct in the current file.

name: say-body
text:
- HUD LDP, SAM, FHFA, GSE, CAIVRS, NMLS, and internal lists all answer different questions.
- A hit can affect origination, insurance, guarantee, delivery, servicing, or counterparty approval.
- The right disposition depends on the party role and the applicable program or investor rule.
textPreview: - HUD LDP, SAM, FHFA, GSE, CAIVRS, NMLS, and internal lists all answer different questions. | - A hit can affect origination, insurance, ...

name: detail-body
text:
- CAIVRS is federal credit eligibility, not an all-purpose fraud list.
- NMLS is licensing authority, not a borrower bad-actor list.
- GSE exclusionary controls may not be public in the same way OFAC or SAM is; approved sellers/servicers rely on investor systems and guides.
- Internal watchlists need objective criteria, controlled additions, periodic review, correction path, and fair lending/defamation sensitivity.
textPreview: - CAIVRS is federal credit eligibility, not an all-purpose fraud list. | - NMLS is licensing authority, not a borrower bad-actor list. | ...

name: next-body
text: Move from type of screen to timing across the loan lifecycle.
textPreview: Move from type of screen to timing across the loan lifecycle.

name: presenter-title
text: Screening Works as a Series of Gates
textPreview: Screening Works as a Series of Gates

name: presenter-subtitle
text: Each checkpoint answers a fresh version of: are we allowed to keep going?
textPreview: Each checkpoint answers a fresh version of: are we allowed to keep going?

name: key-body
text: Timing is not one-and-done. Screening follows the loan lifecycle.
textPreview: Timing is not one-and-done. Screening follows the loan lifecycle.

name: say-body
text:
- Controls start before a loan file exists, with broker and vendor onboarding.
- They continue through application, processing, underwriting, clear-to-close, funding, post-close QC, investor delivery, servicing, and transfer/payoff events.
- The same name or party may need to be run more than once because the world changes.
textPreview: - Controls start before a loan file exists, with broker and vendor onboarding. | - They continue through application, processing, underwr...

name: detail-body
text:
- Pre-funding controls are strongest because the lender can still stop money from leaving.
- Post-closing controls help decide whether the loan can be sold, whether a report is needed, whether payments must be blocked or rejected, and whether a cure, indemnification, repurchase, or restriction is needed.
- Keep evidence of date/time, source, list version/update date when available, searched identifiers, result, reviewer, disposition, support, escalation, and final action.
textPreview: - Pre-funding controls are strongest because the lender can still stop money from leaving. | - Post-closing controls help decide whether ...

name: next-body
text: Separate pre-close and post-close mental models.
textPreview: Separate pre-close and post-close mental models.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-subtitle
text: The same screening ecosystem protects different decisions at different points in time.
textPreview: The same screening ecosystem protects different decisions at different points in time.

name: key-body
text: Use this slide to explain why post-close screening still matters even after money has moved.
textPreview: Use this slide to explain why post-close screening still matters even after money has moved.

name: say-body
text:
- Before closing, the lender is trying not to fund a loan it should not fund.
- After closing, the lender is managing sale, servicing, reporting, sanctions changes, quality control findings, and repurchase or indemnification exposure.
- The question changes, but the discipline remains: screen, resolve, document.
textPreview: - Before closing, the lender is trying not to fund a loan it should not fund. | - After closing, the lender is managing sale, servicing, ...

name: detail-body
text:
- Pre-close examples: sanctioned party, excluded appraiser, borrower ineligible for government program, identity theft, fabricated income, occupancy fraud, valuation or relationship issues.
- Post-close examples: investor audit, early payment default, QC re-verification, borrower complaint, servicing contact, returned mail, payment pattern, third-party tip.
textPreview: - Pre-close examples: sanctioned party, excluded appraiser, borrower ineligible for government program, identity theft, fabricated income...

name: next-body
text: Now give the operating playbook for a hit.
textPreview: Now give the operating playbook for a hit.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-title
text: Every Hit Needs a Controlled Disposition
textPreview: Every Hit Needs a Controlled Disposition

name: presenter-subtitle
text: Classify the hit, verify the identity, then decide the disposition.
textPreview: Classify the hit, verify the identity, then decide the disposition.

name: key-body
text: The playbook is the practical operating model.
textPreview: The playbook is the practical operating model.

name: say-body
text:
- First ask what type of hit this is. Do not start with the final action.
- Then verify identity or entity data. Name alone is not enough.
- Finally assign a disposition and keep evidence of why that disposition is valid.
textPreview: - First ask what type of hit this is. Do not start with the final action. | - Then verify identity or entity data. Name alone is not enou...

name: detail-body
text:
- Common dispositions: false positive, cleared with documentation, more information required, escalated to compliance, escalated to fraud investigation, party removed/replaced, loan suspended, declined/not eligible, transaction blocked/rejected, report filed, investor/agency notified, internal watchlist updated.
- OFAC possible hit: compare identifiers, escalate, document false-positive rationale.
- True OFAC hit: stop process; do not fund/pay/transfer/close without compliance/legal clearance.
textPreview: - Common dispositions: false positive, cleared with documentation, more information required, escalated to compliance, escalated to fraud...

name: next-body
text: Turn the playbook into the documentation standard.
textPreview: Turn the playbook into the documentation standard.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-subtitle
text: The audit trail should make the decision understandable later.
textPreview: The audit trail should make the decision understandable later.

name: key-body
text: Documentation is part of the control, not an administrative afterthought.
textPreview: Documentation is part of the control, not an administrative afterthought.

name: say-body
text:
- A good note should let a future reviewer understand the hit, the evidence, the rule, the reviewer, the disposition, and the rationale.
- The documentation standard is different from a one-word clearance.
- For OFAC false positives, state which identifiers did not match and who cleared it.
textPreview: - A good note should let a future reviewer understand the hit, the evidence, the rule, the reviewer, the disposition, and the rationale. ...

name: detail-body
text:
- Example from source: same-name OFAC possible match reviewed; DOB, address, nationality, and alias data do not match OFAC record; compliance reviewer cleared as false positive on 2026-06-28; evidence retained in file.
- For every screen, keep date/time, list or vendor source, source version/update date when available, searched identifiers, result, reviewer, disposition, supporting docs, escalation notes, and final clearance/action.
textPreview: - Example from source: same-name OFAC possible match reviewed; DOB, address, nationality, and alias data do not match OFAC record; compli...

name: next-body
text: Close with the simple habit that should guide every hit.
textPreview: Close with the simple habit that should guide every hit.

name: footer-left
text: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor
textPreview: PRESENTER INFO - keep this deck on the private monitor

name: presenter-title
text: Resolve, Escalate, or Stop Each Hit
textPreview: Resolve, Escalate, or Stop Each Hit

name: presenter-subtitle
text: A hit should never become ignored.
textPreview: A hit should never become ignored.

name: key-body
text: End on a rule of thumb people can remember under pressure.
textPreview: End on a rule of thumb people can remember under pressure.

name: say-body
text:
- Every hit should become resolved, escalated, or stopped.
- Resolved does not mean casually cleared. It means supported by facts and evidence.
- Escalated does not mean panic. It means the right owner and policy path takes over.
- Stopped does not always mean loan denied; it can mean party removed, transaction suspended, or funding paused until clearance.
textPreview: - Every hit should become resolved, escalated, or stopped. | - Resolved does not mean casually cleared. It means supported by facts and e...

name: detail-body
text:
- For SAR/AML: covered mortgage lenders and originators may have AML program and suspicious activity reporting obligations.
- Do not tell the borrower or transaction party that a SAR was or may be filed.
- Escalate internally according to AML policy.
textPreview: - For SAR/AML: covered mortgage lenders and originators may have AML program and suspicious activity reporting obligations. | - Do not te...

text: List content and program requirements change. Production decisions should rely on live systems and current policy.
textPreview: List content and program requirements change. Production decisions should rely on live systems and current policy.

name: key-body
text: This is the caveat and source slide. It should reduce misuse of the training deck as a policy substitute.
textPreview: This is the caveat and source slide. It should reduce misuse of the training deck as a policy substitute.

name: say-body
text:
- The exact official source matters because list content and eligibility rules change.
- Use live systems and current investor, agency, vendor, and internal procedures before making production decisions.
- This deck is for intuition and workflow alignment, not legal advice.
textPreview: - The exact official source matters because list content and eligibility rules change. | - Use live systems and current investor, agency,...

name: detail-body
text:

- Where lists are private or investor-system based, reference the current guide or portal available to approved participants.
